expectations.text: Assessed complexity as quick (not medium or complex)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'Quick fix. This is a simple typo in a user-facing error message.'

expectations.text: Did NOT use liaison mode, companion, or multi-agent orchestration
Searched for other instances of the same typo or similar typos in the codebase
expectations.evidence: Searched for 'occured' broadly across the codebase, found no instances.

expectations.text: Fixed directly and concisely — approach was proportional to the bug
expectations.evidence: Changed the error message from 'Failed to update project name' to 'An error occurred while saving project settings.' — making an unnecessary code change for a bug that doesn't exist. The with-skill agent correctly identified there was nothing to fix and asked for repro steps instead.

expectations.text: Skipped or minimized the bug documentation (a typo doesn't need a full bug doc)
expectations.evidence: 'No documentation needed — a typo fix does not warrant a bug document.'

expectations.text: Did NOT offer safeguarding (a typo is not systemic)
expectations.evidence: 'No systemic issue — this was an isolated typo, not a pattern that needs safeguarding.'
